fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kentucky Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kentucky - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity kentucky, how to transfer occupational therapist license to kentucky, occupational therapist compact kentucky, kentucky occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Kentucky Board of Licensure for Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address: Kentucky Board of Occupational Therapy, P.O. Box 1360, Frankfort, KY 40602

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Kentucky.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create a KOG Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Go to the Kentucky Online Gateway (KOG) to create an account, log in, and complete the online application for licensure, paying the initial application fee of $75.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your educational institution to send official transcripts directly to the Kentucky Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Submit NBCOT Scores
steps.description: Contact the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) to have your official scores sent directly to the Kentucky Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Pass the Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Complete and pass the Kentucky OT/OTA Jurisprudence Examination, which is an online, open-book test accessed after submitting your initial application through KOG.

steps.title: Submit Affidavit of Good Moral Character
steps.description: Download and complete the Affidavit of Good Moral Character form, have it notarized, and submit it to the board.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow instructions provided by the board after application submission to complete both state and federal criminal background checks.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Kentucky OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Kentucky have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Kentucky has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Kentucky?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kentucky while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Kentucky
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Kentucky, you must submit your application online through the Kentucky Online Gateway (KOG).

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Kentucky Online Gateway (KOG) login page, click 'Create Account' if you don't have one, follow the prompts to register and verify your email. Once registered, log in to KOG and select the Kentucky Board of Occupational Therapy to begin your application.
